meta_title: Roofing in Westgate, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofing in Westgate, FL. CertainTeed shingles & West Lake tile, 140mph wind-rated systems. Free estimates, financing from $87/mo.
h1: Trusted Roofing Replacement & Repair in Westgate, Florida — Built to Handle Whatever the Sky Throws At You
intro:
Westgate is one of Palm Beach County's quietly resilient communities — a neighborhood where working families have put down roots, maintained their properties through decades of South Florida weather, and watched home values climb steadily to an average of around $245,000. But beneath that neighborhood pride lies a real challenge: more than half of all homes in the 33406 ZIP code were built before 1994, meaning they were constructed before the sweeping overhaul of Florida's building codes that followed Hurricane Andrew's devastation. For many Westgate homeowners, the roof over their head is aging, potentially underbuilt for today's wind standards, and quietly draining money through insurance surcharges, energy loss, and deferred maintenance costs.

The Westgate Community sits approximately seven miles from the coastline — far enough to avoid daily salt spray, but absolutely not far enough to escape the fury of a full-scale Florida hurricane. Homes along and around Westgate Park have faced tropical-force winds and sustained storm systems that can compromise even well-maintained roofs. Wind uplift, water intrusion at ridge lines and valleys, and granule loss on aging shingles are common complaints from homeowners in this part of Palm Beach County. When those problems go unaddressed, what starts as a minor roof issue becomes a full ceiling cave-in, mold remediation project, or total loss claim that can take months to resolve.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has completed more than 500 roofing and window projects across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, and we understand the specific demands that Westgate's climate, building age, and neighborhood character place on a roofing system. Whether you're dealing with wind-lifted shingles from the last storm, a roof that failed its 4-point inspection, or a tile system that's simply reached the end of its serviceable life, our team brings 60-plus years of combined installation experience to every job — and we handle every step in-house, no subcontractors involved.

section_why.h2: Why Westgate Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Roofing Projects
section_why.body:
When it comes to roofing in Westgate, the difference between a good experience and a stressful one almost always comes down to who you hire. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've built our reputation across Palm Beach County by doing one thing that too many contractors skip: keeping every project entirely in-house. Our installers are our employees, not day laborers or outsourced crews. That means the same standard of quality you see on day one carries through to the final nail and the final inspection. We don't pass your project off to a stranger with a truck and a staple gun.

For Westgate specifically, our experience with older housing stock is especially important. Because 57% of homes in this area predate 1994, we regularly encounter roof decks, framing configurations, and drainage designs that require experienced hands and real problem-solving skills — not just a cookie-cutter installation routine. Our team evaluates each home individually, identifying any structural concerns with the deck, assessing the condition of existing underlayment, and recommending the right product match based on the home's pitch, style, and exposure level. We pull all necessary permits through the Palm Beach County Building Division and manage the inspection process from start to finish so you never have to chase down a paperwork trail.

Our 60-plus years of combined team experience also means we've seen what works and what fails in this region's storm environment. We've installed roofs that have since survived multiple named storms. We've also re-roofed homes that were failed by cheap materials or improper installation from less experienced crews. That track record matters, and it's why we've earned an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau and consistently receive five-star reviews from homeowners across West Palm Beach, Palm Springs, and Lake Clarke Shores — communities that share Westgate's wind exposure and housing profile.

Finally, we believe that protecting your home shouldn't require depleting your savings account. We offer flexible financing starting as low as $87 per month with $0-down options available for qualified homeowners. Getting a new hurricane-rated roof in Westgate has never been more accessible, and our team will walk you through every financing path so you can make the decision that's right for your household.

section_history.h2: Westgate's Storm History Makes a Strong Case for a Hurricane-Rated Roof
section_history.body:
Westgate has seen its share of serious weather over the past three decades. Since 1990, this part of Palm Beach County has been impacted by seven named storms, including three that left lasting marks on the community: Hurricane Frances in 2004, Hurricane Wilma in 2005, and Hurricane Irma in 2017. Frances was a slow-moving, water-heavy storm that sat over South Florida for hours, exploiting any roof penetration or aging sealant to push water deep into attic spaces and ceilings. Many Westgate homeowners who thought their roofs were fine discovered otherwise when Frances finally moved through.

The following year, Wilma struck with some of the highest wind speeds ever recorded in Palm Beach County, causing widespread shingle blow-off and tile displacement across neighborhoods throughout the region. For older homes in Westgate — many of which were built with roofing systems not originally designed to meet post-Andrew wind codes — Wilma was particularly punishing. Homeowners who had avoided re-roofing through the mid-2000s found themselves filing large insurance claims and waiting months for qualified contractors in a post-storm market flooded with demand and short on reliable crews.

Hurricane Irma in 2017 reinforced the same lesson. Though Irma weakened before its inland track, its sustained tropical-storm to hurricane-force winds still caused significant roof damage throughout Palm Beach County. Ridge caps, flashings, and aging three-tab shingles proved especially vulnerable. For many Westgate residents, Irma was the storm that finally prompted a long-overdue roof replacement. If your home has not had its roof evaluated since Irma, now is the time — because the next named storm is not a matter of if, but when.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements Every Westgate Homeowner Should Know Before Re-Roofing
section_code.body:
Although Westgate is not located within Florida's High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation that applies to Miami-Dade and Broward counties with the most stringent requirements in the state — Palm Beach County still mandates hurricane-rated roofing products under the Florida Building Code. Homes in the 33406 ZIP code are classified in a 140-mph wind design zone, which means every product used in a roofing installation — from shingles and tile to underlayment and fasteners — must be rated to withstand those forces and carry the appropriate product approval for use in Palm Beach County.

For shingle roofs, this typically means using products with a Florida Product Approval (FL#) that demonstrates compliance with ASTM D3161 Class F or ASTM D7158 Class H wind resistance testing. For tile systems, products must meet impact and uplift standards appropriate for the 140-mph zone. Underlayment requirements are also more stringent than in other parts of the country — Florida Building Code requires a secondary water barrier in most Palm Beach County roofing applications, which dramatically reduces the risk of interior water damage during a storm even if the primary roofing material is compromised.

All roofing work in Westgate requires a permit pulled through the Palm Beach County Building Division, followed by a mandatory inspection after installation. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages this entire process for our customers — we file the permit application, schedule the inspections, and ensure every product we install carries the appropriate approvals. We do not perform unpermitted work, and we strongly advise Westgate homeowners to be cautious of any contractor who suggests skipping the permit process. Unpermitted roofing work can create serious problems when you sell your home, file an insurance claim, or need to prove code compliance for a wind mitigation inspection.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Architectural Shingles and West Lake Tile: The Right Products for Westgate Roofs
section_products.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we don't install just any roofing product — we've specifically chosen CertainTeed and West Lake as our primary roofing brands because they consistently outperform alternatives in South Florida's demanding climate. For Westgate homeowners choosing a shingle roof, we recommend CertainTeed's Landmark Series architectural shingles. The Landmark Series is one of the most recognized and tested shingle systems in the country, featuring a dual-layer construction that provides superior wind resistance, dimensional aesthetics, and a Class A fire rating. The Landmark Series carries product approvals for use in Palm Beach County's 140-mph wind zone and comes with robust warranty coverage including a Lifetime Limited Warranty on the shingle itself and a 10-year SureStart Protection period for full replacement cost coverage.

For homeowners with tile roofs — either existing systems that need full replacement or new installations that want the premium look and longevity that tile provides — we work with West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. West Lake tile is engineered for Florida's heat, rain, and hurricane exposure, and their products carry appropriate Florida Product Approvals for wind zones matching Westgate's classification. Concrete tile from West Lake offers exceptional impact resistance and thermal mass, helping keep homes cooler during the long South Florida summer. Clay tile provides the traditional barrel or flat profile that many Palm Beach County homeowners prefer, with a service life that can exceed 50 years when properly installed.

Both product lines are installed using manufacturer-specified fastening and underlayment systems that meet or exceed Florida Building Code requirements. We never take shortcuts on installation details like hip and ridge fastening, valley flashing, or perimeter edge metal — these are the areas where roofs most commonly fail in high-wind events, and we treat them with the attention they deserve.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in Westgate, and Is It Worth the Investment?
section_cost.body:
For a home in Westgate valued around $245,000, a full roof replacement represents a meaningful investment — but also one of the highest-return home improvements you can make in Palm Beach County. Depending on your home's square footage, roof pitch, existing damage, and the product system you choose, a typical full shingle replacement in the 33406 area might range from $9,000 to $16,000, while a tile replacement can range from $14,000 to $25,000 or more for larger homes. These figures include materials, labor, permit fees, and disposal of your old roofing system. Because we handle all work in-house, our pricing reflects actual project costs rather than the markups that general contractors add when outsourcing to subcontractors.

For many Westgate homeowners, the sticker price of a new roof is the first number they see — but it's rarely the most important one. A new hurricane-rated roof can reduce your homeowner's insurance premium by $800 to $2,000 or more per year through wind mitigation credits, a significant annual saving that compounds over the life of the roof. Additionally, a new roof typically adds 15 to 20 percent to a home's resale value and removes a significant barrier to closing for buyers who require financing. In a market where lenders and insurers are increasingly scrutinizing roof age and condition, having a code-compliant, permitted, newly installed roof is a genuine competitive advantage.

To make roofing accessible for Westgate families, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. Our financing partners offer a range of term lengths and rate structures, and our team can help you model the true monthly cost against your anticipated insurance savings so you can see the real bottom line from day one.

section_insurance.h2: How a New Hurricane-Rated Roof Can Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance in Westgate
section_insurance.body:
Palm Beach County homeowners have felt the full weight of Florida's insurance market instability over the past several years — carriers leaving the state, premiums rising dramatically, and insurers scrutinizing roof age and condition more closely than ever. In Westgate, where a significant portion of homes carry roofs that are 20 or more years old, many homeowners are facing non-renewal notices or coverage restrictions specifically tied to their roof's age and condition. A new roof is one of the most direct and powerful ways to stabilize your insurance situation and access the best available premium rates.

Florida law requires insurance carriers to give credit for wind mitigation features verified through a licensed wind mitigation inspection. When you install a new roof using hurricane-rated products with a secondary water barrier, enhanced fastening patterns, and code-compliant hip-to-ridge construction, you create the conditions for meaningful credits across multiple categories on the wind mitigation form — including roof covering, roof deck attachment, roof-to-wall connection, and roof shape. Depending on your carrier and your home's specific construction, these credits can reduce the wind portion of your premium by 30 to 45 percent, translating to hundreds or thousands of dollars in annual savings.

Common insurers serving the Westgate area, including Citizens Property Insurance and various admitted carriers operating in Palm Beach County, use the wind mitigation form as a primary pricing tool. When Premier Impact Windows & Roofing completes your roof installation, we build it to the specifications that maximize your wind mitigation credits — and we encourage all customers to schedule a licensed wind mitigation inspection with an independent inspector shortly after the final building inspection clears. That report becomes your documentation for savings that will follow you for the life of the roof.

faqs.q: My Westgate home has an older tile roof — can it be repaired or does it need full replacement?
faqs.a: It depends on the extent and type of damage. If your tile roof is fewer than 20 years old, has isolated cracked or slipped tiles, and the underlayment is still intact, targeted repairs may be sufficient. However, many older tile roofs in Westgate — particularly those installed before 1994 — have underlayment that has deteriorated past its service life, which means water is getting in even when visible tile damage looks minor. We offer thorough roof evaluations to give you an honest assessment rather than defaulting to the most expensive recommendation.

faqs.q: How much does roofing financing actually cost per month for a Westgate home?
faqs.a: Financing starts at $87 per month with $0 down for qualified applicants. Your actual monthly payment will depend on the project total, the term length you select, and your credit profile. Many Westgate homeowners find that when they factor in the insurance savings a new hurricane-rated roof generates — often $100 to $200 per month — the net out-of-pocket cost of financing is minimal or even cash-flow positive from year one.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my roof in Westgate, and how does that process work?
faqs.a: Yes. All roofing replacements in Westgate require a permit pulled through the Palm Beach County Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process — application, fee payment, and scheduling the required post-installation inspection. We never perform unpermitted work. Unpermitted roofing can affect your ability to sell your home, file an insurance claim, or qualify for wind mitigation credits, so this step is non-negotiable for your protection.

faqs.q: How long does a full roof replacement typically take for a home in Westgate?
faqs.a: For most single-family homes in the 33406 area, a full shingle roof replacement takes one to two days for the physical installation. Tile roof replacements may take two to four days depending on the size and complexity of the home. Add permit lead time — typically five to fifteen business days in Palm Beach County — plus scheduling. In total, from signed contract to completed inspection, most homeowners should plan for a two-to-four week timeline under normal conditions.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Westgate?
faqs.a: For most Westgate homeowners, yes — and often significantly. A new roof installed with a secondary water barrier, hurricane-rated shingles or tile, and proper fastening patterns qualifies for wind mitigation credits under Florida law. These credits can reduce the wind portion of your premium by 30 to 45 percent depending on your carrier and home construction. Many homeowners in Palm Beach County save $800 to $2,000 or more annually after re-roofing. We recommend scheduling a wind mitigation inspection immediately after your roof passes its building inspection.

cta_headline: Ready to Protect Your Westgate Home With a Hurricane-Rated Roof? Let's Talk.
cta_body: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is ready to evaluate your Westgate home and give you an honest, no-pressure assessment — reach out today for your free roofing estimate and discover how easy it is to get started with financing from just $87 a month.
